transcript.clean_text: day two of para nice saw the rinds head southeast from the start in Shiraz taking three categorized climbs two intermediate sprints and 166 and a half kilometers before they eventually arrived at the finish in Charlotte Searle won once again though it was the weather and in particular the wind that was expected to play as much of a part in the outcome as the terrain itself it was under blue skies that the riders set off this morning Shakman and alle philippe leading the way in the yellow and green jerseys respectively but the weather unexpectedly soon took a turn for the worse kom leader Jonathan Iver of total Direct Energy and hosing Manuel Diaz of Nippo Delta Provence were the only two rides in the days break their advantage down to less than a minute and a half of just under half the stage remaining behind them at this point the whole peloton was still together but it was only a matter of time before the proverbial would hit the fan and those same nerves in the bunch that we saw yesterday led to the break being caught 216 k's further down the road at the head of affairs at this point was the team of race leader max ahmed for a hands girl three-time world champion peters again was unusually on lead-out duties at this point in the race shepherding the german towards the intermediate sprint where he duly picked up three bonus seconds second there though went to colombian champion Sergio hikita the Sun reemerged very briefly inside the last 30 kilometers but the race was very much on as it was for most of the day Sam Bennett who now rides for de Kooning quickstep wanted the rise to be caught on the wrong side of a split in the crosswinds and it was turning into a repeat of yesterday and that meant more crashes this time for nairo quintana he was quickly back onto his brothers bike that was the worst time for this to happen with the front group now riding flat out at this stage of the race we had a group of around 30 rides at the front but as the kilometers ticked down so too did the number of riders it was certainly one of the hardest days of racing that we've seen so far in 2020 although it can be a frustrating race for general classification riders with their hopes often - before they've even seen a mountain world champion mass Patterson was loving it though doing a huge job to keep his GCE different Enzo nearly at the head of affairs racing really doesn't get much better than this at least as a spectator rides and groups all over the road and we still had 10 kilometers to go the final split involved just 13 riders he Gaeta Shakman kneeble e-cig an Ackerman pettersen Paulette nude Zola sterben gross Charlotte Neelam's and verse Schmitt and also Maspeth ursin still riding his heart out at the front amongst those caught behind with thibaut pinot and roman bard a the latter had already lost a chunk of time yesterday but Pinot is still in the hunt for the GC so his group Armour FDJ team was doing their best to limit the losses along with the GC battle there was of course a stage win up for grabs you don't often see a race leader driving things on the front but that's exactly what Shakman was doing in part to increase the advantage in parts of work for their spring - Pascal Ackerman he would do his last hard pull on the front just inside the closing kilometre of the stage in the sprint itself though it was the Gann who taken over the lead out duties Ackerman on his wheel but behind him ntt sprinted Jack Ohman at Zola was waiting in the wings Ackerman left it as long as he possibly could kick him for the line with a hundred and fifty meters to go but he didn't have the legs and it Zola did though the attain had more in enough in a tank to come round Ackerman and take his second win of the season so far here's the overhead shot needs ola showing a clean pair of wheels to everybody else a command holding on for second place yes purse Durbin continued his run of good form with third as the Italian national team managed khazzani said on Twitter a win for an Italian from Lombardi giving us all a reason to smile and well deserved to ntt have now won more races this year than in the whole of the 2019 season and Zola himself really seems to have stepped up to another level the GC leader remains the same though max Ackman back on the podium in yellow and with a 15 second advantage over knit solo who's moved himself up to second pig eater the best of the GC prospect City in fourth at 23 seconds whilst Neely is a further 5 seconds back in 9th place tomorrow it is the second longest stage of Perry nice at 212 and a half kilometers it starts where today finished in Charlotte Searle one they headed a predominately southwesterly direction towards the finish line English at and with the wind blown for the Northwest this is going to be another long and tense day in the peloton now our prediction yesterday was wrong in more ways than one but tomorrow I'm hoping to improve at least by getting the correct team name I'm going for Caleb Ewan of lotto suit out give us your predictions in the comment section below and make sure you tune back in tomorrow what should be another very entertaining stage
